name: Camera2d

unions.name: Camera2d
unions.comment:
A Camera onto a 2d scene, centered on a particular origin point in the scene
and with a zoom ratio.

unions.name: ZoomSpace
unions.comment:
ZoomSpace is a mapping of the camera coordinates from (X, Y, Zoom) to
(X, Y, 1 / Zoom). Linear motion of the camera in ZoomSpace maps to linear motion
of the camera in the 3d space above the drawing.

The camera can be thought of as looking down from above onto a drawing on the 2d
XY-plane at Z = 0. The camera has a height above this plane and always keeps the
plane in perfect focus. As the cameras height changes, so does its degree of zoom.

ZoomSpace is linear with respect to this 3d space above the drawing. Linear
motion of the camera in ZoomSpace will produce linear motion of the camera
as imagined this way, and is useful for getting predictable animations of the
camera position and zoom level.

values.name: fromZoomSpace
values.type: Point3d.Point3d units (Camera2d.ZoomSpace screenUnits coordinates) -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates

values.name: interpolateFrom
values.comment: Interpolate between one camera position and another in [ZoomSpace](#ZoomSpace).
values.type: Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Basics.Float -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates

values.name: origin
values.comment: Gets the camera origin point in scene coordinates.
values.type: Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Point2d.Point2d units coordinates

values.name: pointToScene
values.comment:
Maps a point in screen space to a point in scene space.

This can be useful when mapping pointer events onto a drawing, since the pointer
events will be described by their screen coordinates.
values.type: Camera2d.Camera2d sceneUnits screenUnits sceneCoordinates -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> Point2d.Point2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> Point2d.Point2d sceneUnits sceneCoordinates

values.name: pointToScreen
values.comment:
Maps a point in scene space to a point in screen space.

This can be useful when overlaying something in screen space onto a drawing in scene
space, for example a menu or other user interface construction that is not part of
the drawing itself.
values.type: Camera2d.Camera2d sceneUnits screenUnits sceneCoordinates -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> Point2d.Point2d sceneUnits sceneCoordinates -> Point2d.Point2d screenUnits screenCoordinates

values.name: setOrigin
values.comment: Shifts the camera origin point to a new location in scene coordinates.
values.type: Point2d.Point2d units coordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates

values.name: setZoom
values.comment:
Adjusts the camera zoom level.

The origin point will not be shifted by this operation. The effect of this on
the screen mapping will be that the zoom is centered on the middle of the
screen, and the origin point remains at the center of the screen.
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float (Quantity.Rate screenUnits units) -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates

values.name: setZoomAtScreenPoint
values.comment:
Adjusts the camera zoom around a point on the screen. The point within the
scene corresponding to the screen point, will be at the same screen point after
the zoom adjustment.

This is convenient when adjusting the zoom around a pointer position, allowing
a user to zoom in on a particular area under the pointer.

Note that if the screen point does not align with the origin point, then the
origin point will be shifted by this operation.
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float (Quantity.Rate screenUnits units) -> Point2d.Point2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates

values.name: svgViewBox
values.comment:
Given a camera and a rectangular frame where a drawing will be rendered on
screen, provides the SVG `viewBox` parameters that will yield a correctly scaled
and translated scene space described by that camera.

The screen coordinate defined frame will have the cameras origin point at its
center, and the frame will be the same size as the whole SVG element. See
[svgViewBoxWithFocus](#svgViewBoxWithFocus) for a version where the camera is
centered in a different frame.
values.type: Camera2d.Camera2d sceneUnits screenUnits sceneCoordinates -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> TypedSvg.Core.Attribute a

values.name: svgViewBoxWithFocus
values.comment:
Given a camera and a rectangular frame where a drawing will be rendered on
screen, provides the SVG `viewBox` parameters that will yield a correctly scaled
and translated scene space described by that camera.

In this case two screen coordinate defined frames are provided to set up the camera.
The first defines the area where the camera is considered to be focussed and centered
on, but this does not have to occupy the whole of the SVG element. The second defines
the boundary of the actual SVG element. Typically the first frame is wholly inside
the second one, but it does not have to be.

This is useful if you want a camera that does not fill all of an SVG, you might have
areas that show other things or some kind of overlay that you consider to be acting
as a boundary to the camera frame.

If you have an SVG that fills the whole `window`, and want the camera focussed on a
`frame` inside that, this would give you the correct SVG viewBox parameters:

    Camera2d.svgViewBoxWithFocus
        camera
        frame
        window
values.type: Camera2d.Camera2d sceneUnits screenUnits sceneCoordinates -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> TypedSvg.Core.Attribute a

values.name: toZoomSpace
values.comment:
Maps the camera coords to ZoomSpace.

This is useful in conjunction with [fromZoomSpace](#fromZoomSpace) when animating the
camera. See [ZoomSpace](#ZoomSpace) for an explanation of the geometry of ZoomSpace
and how it simplifies the camera motion.
values.type: Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Point3d.Point3d units (Camera2d.ZoomSpace screenUnits coordinates)

values.name: translateBy
values.comment: Shifts the camera by a vector in screne coordinates.
values.type: Vector2d.Vector2d units coordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates

values.name: translateByScreenVector
values.comment:
Shifts the camera origin point by a vector in screen coordinates.

This can be convenient when working with pointers on the screen.
values.type: Vector2d.Vector2d screenUnits screenCoordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates

values.name: zoom
values.comment: Gets the cameras current zoom level.
values.type: Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float (Quantity.Rate screenUnits units)

values.name: zoomedAt
values.comment: Creates a camera centered at the origin point with the given zoom ratio.
values.type: Point2d.Point2d units coordinates -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float (Quantity.Rate screenUnits units) -> Camera2d.Camera2d units screenUnits coordinates
